cloudlex.name: CloudLex
cloudlex.short_description: Cloud case management platform built specifically for personal injury law firms, combining intake, matters, and built-in AI.
cloudlex.headquarters: New York City, NY, United States

features.description: Shareable intake forms and lead capture with one-click conversion of leads into matters.
features.title: Intake management

features.description: Centralizes case tasks, deadlines, documents, and contacts in a single record per case.
features.title: Matter management

features.description: Organizes and shares case documents with AI-driven search across files.

features.description: Shared visibility into court dates and statute-of-limitations deadlines across the firm.
features.title: Calendar and deadline tracking

features.description: Requests and tracks medical records as part of the case file.
features.title: Medical records tracking

features.description: Tracks demands, offers, and fee calculations through case resolution.
features.title: Settlement management

features.description: Built-in AI for demand drafting, medical record summaries, intake chatbots, and case Q&A.
features.title: Lexee AI

features.description: Add-on services including records retrieval, chronologies, document indexing, and a 24/7 bilingual call center.
features.title: Paralegal services

pros: Purpose-built for personal injury practice rather than general legal work
Built-in Lexee AI handles demand drafting and medical summaries
Optional paralegal services add records retrieval and a 24/7 bilingual call center
Integrates with Microsoft 365, Gmail, Google Calendar, DocuSign, and Adobe Sign
Hosted on Microsoft Azure with encryption and multi-factor authentication
cons: Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Focused on personal injury, so less suited to firms handling other practice areas
No free trial or free plan information available on the website

faqs.answer: CloudLex is case management software focused specifically on personal injury law firms, covering intake through settlement.
faqs.question: What is CloudLex used for?

faqs.answer: Yes, its Lexee AI component handles demand drafting, medical record summaries, intake chatbots, and case Q&A.
faqs.question: Does CloudLex include AI features?

faqs.answer: It integrates with Microsoft Office 365, Gmail, Google Calendar, DocuSign, and Adobe Sign.
faqs.question: What does CloudLex integrate with?

faqs.answer: No, pricing is not listed on the CloudLex website; firms need to contact the company directly.
faqs.question: Is CloudLex pricing public?

faqs.answer: CloudLex's offices are located in New York City.
faqs.question: Where is CloudLex based?

uservoice.name: UserVoice

Enterprise-level volume and custom features require contacting sales

faqs.answer: FullEnrich is a B2B contact enrichment platform that waterfalls over 20 data providers to find and verify email addresses and phone numbers.
faqs.question: What is FullEnrich?

faqs.answer: FullEnrich's Pro plan is €55/month for 1,000 credits, with a free trial of 50 credits and no credit card required.
faqs.question: How much does FullEnrich cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, FullEnrich supports Claude via MCP and OpenAI-based workflows, in addition to Zapier, Make, n8n, and Clay.
faqs.question: Does FullEnrich integrate with AI agents?

faqs.answer: FullEnrich states it is SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR and CCPA compliant.
faqs.question: Is FullEnrich compliant with data regulations?

features: Work, mobile, and personal email enrichment

corteza-crm.name: Corteza CRM
corteza-crm.short_description: Corteza is an open-source, low-code platform (Apache 2.0) with a built-in CRM module for records-based business apps.

features.description: Gives a 360-degree overview of leads and accounts, with a module for automating sales and service processes.
features.title: Low-code CRM

features.description: A Google Workspace-style collaboration layer bundled into the platform.
features.title: Unified Workspace

features.description: Built-in Slack-style internal messaging for teams.
features.title: Enterprise Messaging

features.description: Role-based access control that lets organizations apply complex internal security policies.
features.title: Flattened RBAC

features.description: Build and iterate custom records-based business apps beyond CRM.
features.title: Low-code app builder

features.description: The platform is built to conform to WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines.
features.title: Accessibility

pros: Fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license with no vendor lock-in
Code is contributed to the Commons Conservancy Software Foundation, so access cannot be revoked by a single vendor
Bundles CRM, internal messaging, and workspace tools in one self-hosted platform
Actively maintained on GitHub with recent commits and 2,000+ stars
cons: Requires self-hosting (e.g. via Docker) and technical setup to run
No first-party managed-cloud pricing page on cortezaproject.org; hosted instances are offered by third parties such as Planet Crust
Smaller community and ecosystem than major commercial CRMs like Salesforce

faqs.answer: Yes. Corteza is fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to download and self-host.
faqs.question: Is Corteza free?

faqs.answer: Corteza is released under the Apache License 2.0.
faqs.question: What license does Corteza use?

faqs.answer: Corteza is designed to be self-hosted via Docker; hosted cloud instances are also offered by third parties such as Planet Crust, separate from the core open-source project.
faqs.question: Can I run Corteza without managing my own server?

faqs.answer: No. Corteza is a broader low-code platform that includes a CRM module alongside a unified workspace and enterprise messaging.
faqs.question: Is Corteza only a CRM?

atomic-crm.name: Atomic CRM
atomic-crm.short_description: Atomic CRM is an open-source CRM toolkit by Marmelab, built with React, Shadcn UI, and Supabase, that teams can self-host or customize.

features.description: Customizable Kanban board for tracking deals through a sales pipeline.
features.title: Sales pipeline and Kanban board

features.description: Contact organization with avatars, activity tracking, notes, and email-to-note capture.
features.title: Contact and activity management

features.description: Task creation with reminders tied to contacts and deals.
features.title: Task management

features.description: Import and export CRM data for migrations and reporting.
features.title: Data import/export

features.description: SSO support via Google, Azure, Keycloak, or Auth0.
features.title: Single sign-on

features.description: API integration plus an MCP Server for connecting AI assistants to CRM data.
features.title: API and MCP Server

features.description: A companion mobile app for accessing the CRM on the go.
features.title: Mobile app

pros: Fully open source with code available on GitHub, so any feature can be modified
No vendor lock-in — can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure at no cost
Built on a modern, well-documented stack (React, TypeScript, Shadcn UI, Supabase)
Includes SSO and an MCP Server for AI-assistant integration out of the box
cons: Paid Starter and Team plans cap contacts and storage (1,000 contacts/1GB and 10,000 contacts/10GB respectively)
Self-hosting requires managing your own Supabase/PostgreSQL infrastructure
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published

faqs.answer: Yes, it can be self-hosted for free since it is fully open source; Marmelab also offers paid managed plans starting at €25/month.
faqs.question: Is Atomic CRM free?

faqs.answer: Atomic CRM is built by Marmelab, using React, TypeScript, Shadcn UI, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase.
faqs.question: Who built Atomic CRM?

faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting is supported for full data sovereignty, in addition to Marmelab's Supabase-managed hosting option.
faqs.question: Can I self-host Atomic CRM?

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports SSO via Google, Azure, Keycloak, and Auth0.
faqs.question: Does Atomic CRM support single sign-on?
